title: arnoglossum ovatum var. lanceolatum
intro: Arnoglossum ovatum var. lanceolatum, also known as Lance-leaved Arnoglossum or Lance-leaved Green-headed Sneezeweed, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in dry to moist prairies, and open woods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, where its leaves and roots are known for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. These medicinal properties translate to several health benefits, including the relief of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential in treating skin irritations and wounds. Arnoglossum ovatum var. lanceolatum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
